profile.name: Robert T. Bigelow
roles: Founder, National Institute for Discovery Science (NIDS)
Founder, Bigelow Aerospace / Bigelow Aerospace Advanced Space Studies (BAASS)
organizations: National Institute for Discovery Science (NIDS) — private research organization (1995-2005)
Bigelow Aerospace Advanced Space Studies (BAASS) — government contractor (2007-2012)
Budget Suites of America (real estate, primary wealth source)
profile.clearance: Facility clearance held by BAASS for administration of the $22M AAWSAP Defense Intelligence Agency contract; Bigelow personally held appropriate access levels during government contracting period
profile.summary: Robert Bigelow is the Las Vegas real estate billionaire whose private funding and government contracting activities represent the single most significant private investment in classified UAP research in American history. Beginning in 1995 with the founding of NIDS — a private research organization staffed with credentialed scientists and former intelligence officials — Bigelow sustained the organizational infrastructure that connected the earlier UAP research community (the Lazar and Knapp era, Las Vegas-based) with the modern disclosure ecosystem (Puthoff, Davis, AATIP, AAWSAP). His company BAASS received the $22 million DIA AAWSAP contract in 2007 and produced 38 classified Defense Intelligence Reference Documents. In a May 2017 CBS 60 Minutes interview, he stated he is 'absolutely convinced' that extraterrestrials exist and have visited Earth. Bigelow is not a government whistleblower; he is the institutional patron whose resources and organizational network made modern UAP research infrastructure possible. His role in the history of U.S. UAP investigation is documented and verifiable through government contracts, published research, and on-record statements.
background: Born Las Vegas, Nevada (May 8, 1944); developed early interest in UAP through family accounts of a roadside encounter in Nevada
Built Budget Suites of America into a major extended-stay hotel chain across the Sun Belt; net worth estimated at $700M+ at peak
Funded UAP research as a personal conviction rather than a commercial venture: 'I've been a researcher and student of UFOs for many, many years'
Primary research base was always Nevada — the same geographic environment as the S-4/Area 51 complex documented by Lazar, and the Las Vegas media market where George Knapp operated

key_events.event: Born Las Vegas, Nevada; family accounts of a UAP roadside sighting in Nevada later described as formative influence on his interest
Begins privately funding UAP and parapsychology research; contacts researchers in the intelligence community and science community to assess what is known

Founds the National Institute for Discovery Science (NIDS) in Las Vegas with a reported $10 million initial endowment; recruits credentialed scientists, former DIA and FBI personnel, and investigators including Colm Kelleher (Research Director) and John Alexander

Purchases Skinwalker Ranch (also known as Sherman Ranch) in Uintah Basin, Utah from the Sherman family for $200,000; establishes it as a live-in research site for anomalous phenomena; installs cameras, sensors, and a resident team
Founds Bigelow Aerospace in Las Vegas to develop inflatable space habitat technology (BEAM modules); creates the organizational infrastructure that later becomes BAASS

NIDS conducts and publishes its Skinwalker Ranch investigation findings; George Knapp and Colm Kelleher co-author 'Hunt for the Skinwalker' (2005) documenting the research; NIDS formally dissolves in 2004, with activities transitioning to BAASS

BAASS (Bigelow Aerospace Advanced Space Studies) wins the Defense Intelligence Agency's AAWSAP (Advanced Aerospace Weapon System Applications Program) contract worth $10 million per year for two years ($22 million total); staffs the program with approximately 50 researchers including Hal Puthoff, Eric Davis, and others from the EarthTech network

BAASS administers AAWSAP, producing 38 classified Defense Intelligence Reference Documents covering warp drives, traversable wormholes, antigravity, remote viewing, and related topics; also conducts field investigations of reported UAP encounters including Skinwalker Ranch incidents
Sells Skinwalker Ranch to Brandon Fugal for a reported $4.5 million after 20 years of research; the property subsequently becomes the subject of a History Channel documentary series

CBS 60 Minutes interview: states 'I'm absolutely convinced' that ETs exist and have visited Earth; says he doesn't care about others' skepticism; describes having spent 'millions and millions' on research. The interview airs weeks before the NYT AATIP story breaks.
New York Times AATIP investigation names BAASS as the contractor that housed the program; publicly confirms the Bigelow-DIA-AATIP connection that had previously been hidden from public view

Continues Bigelow Aerospace operations focused on inflatable space habitats; periodically referenced in UAP disclosure discussions as the institutional foundation connecting the 1990s private research network to the modern disclosure era

nids.funding: Private — Robert Bigelow personal endowment (estimated $10M+ over lifespan)
nids.mission: Scientific investigation of anomalous phenomena including UAP, cattle mutilations, crop circles, and related low-frequency events. NIDS maintained a scientific advisory board and pursued peer-reviewed methodology where possible.
notable_members: John B. Alexander — former Army Colonel, non-lethal weapons researcher
Eric W. Davis — visiting research scientist (astrophysicist, EarthTech)
George Knapp — media partner; investigative journalist, KLAS-TV Las Vegas
Jacques Vallée — scientific advisor; ufologist and computer scientist
Hal Puthoff — scientific advisor; physicist (EarthTech International)
key_outputs: Ongoing UAP incident database (not publicly released)
Cattle mutilation research and published papers
Contributed personnel and research framework to BAASS/AAWSAP
nids.significance: NIDS was the organizational bridge between the 1990s Las Vegas-centered UAP research community (George Knapp, Bob Lazar era) and the post-2007 government contracting ecosystem. Both Eric Davis (AAWSAP DIRD author) and George Knapp (Lazar's original journalist) worked within the NIDS network before and during the AAWSAP period.

baass_aawsap.program: Advanced Aerospace Weapon System Applications Program (AAWSAP)
baass_aawsap.contract_value: $22 million over two years (DIA funding; secured by Senator Harry Reid)
baass_aawsap.period: 2007–2012 (contract ran two years; follow-on period of activity continued)
baass_aawsap.staff: Approximately 50 researchers drawn from EarthTech International, NIDS alumni, and the broader exotic physics research community
products: 38 classified Defense Intelligence Reference Documents (DIRDs) covering: warp drives, traversable wormholes, antigravity, nuclear powered aircraft, metallic glasses, SETI, and unconventional aircraft performance
Field investigation reports on reported UAP encounters submitted to DIA
Skinwalker Ranch anomaly documentation submitted as part of AAWSAP deliverables
baass_aawsap.congressional_nexus: Senator Harry Reid (D-NV) secured the initial AAWSAP appropriation, describing Bigelow as the ideal contractor given his prior research infrastructure. This was an earmark-style appropriation.
baass_aawsap.ttsa_connection: BAASS's AAWSAP work directly preceded and informed AATIP; when Luis Elizondo's team at the Pentagon took over the UAP investigation program circa 2010, they were building on the BAASS-produced DIRD research base. Elizondo, Puthoff, and Mellon all operated in this ecosystem.
baass_aawsap.declassified_dird_note: The 38 DIRDs were partially released via FOIA in 2021-2022, confirming BAASS authorship and the scope of the research commissioned.

phenomena_reported: UAP sightings — multiple craft types including large luminous objects
Cattle mutilations — approximately 14 incidents documented on the property
Poltergeist activity — objects moved, voices heard, equipment malfunctioning
Telepathic-style experiences reported by on-site researchers
Interdimensional portal phenomena — researchers reported seeing a tunnel-shaped space through which animals or objects apparently transited
skinwalker_ranch.research_approach: NIDS installed cameras, motion sensors, and stationed resident researchers including Colm Kelleher. The ranch was treated as a living laboratory. A scientific advisory board reviewed findings.
skinwalker_ranch.published_account: 'Hunt for the Skinwalker' — George Knapp and Colm Kelleher (2005). First public account of the NIDS Skinwalker investigation. Sold well and brought the ranch into mainstream UAP awareness.
skinwalker_ranch.notes: The Skinwalker Ranch phenomena are categorically different from the UAP crash-retrieval narratives that dominate the rest of DECUR's data. DECUR includes them because Bigelow's Skinwalker research and AAWSAP were institutionally connected, and because Eric Davis (a DECUR insider) was a NIDS visiting scientist during the Skinwalker period.

claims.claim: Extraterrestrial intelligence exists and has visited Earth
claims.basis: Stated explicitly on CBS 60 Minutes in May 2017: 'I'm absolutely convinced. That's all there is to it.' His conviction is based on decades of private research funding rather than a single observation.
claims.notes: Bigelow is the most publicly explicit major UAP funder to make this claim directly on mainstream television; unlike most insiders, he did not hedge or qualify the statement

claims.claim: The U.S. government has been in possession of recovered non-human materials
claims.basis: Strongly implied in multiple interviews; his BAASS AAWSAP contract included investigation of retrieved materials as part of scope. Has not provided documented evidence publicly.
claims.notes: Corroborated indirectly by Eric Davis and David Grusch making similar claims independently; Bigelow's institutional position would have given him exposure to any government-side material evidence

claims.claim: BAASS and NIDS research documented genuine anomalous phenomena beyond conventional explanation
claims.basis: BAASS delivered 38 classified DIRDs to DIA — a government agency accepted and classified these documents, implying institutional legitimacy. The Skinwalker Ranch phenomena are documented in a published book co-authored by an investigative journalist.
claims.notes: The DIRDs themselves have been partially released via FOIA, confirming the program's scope. Whether the phenomena documented are 'genuine' vs. misidentified conventional phenomena is not resolvable from public evidence.

claims.claim: Extraterrestrial life exists in the near-Earth environment and is not a distant future discovery
claims.basis: Stated in 60 Minutes and multiple other interviews; no publicly verifiable supporting evidence beyond his personal conviction derived from private research
claims.notes: His specific phrasing on 60 Minutes was 'right under people's noses' — implying active proximity, not just distant existence

supporting: Documented government contracting history — BAASS as the DIA AAWSAP contractor is confirmed by FOIA releases and multiple government officials; this is not in dispute

Recruited genuinely credentialed researchers — Puthoff, Davis, Kelleher, and Vallée all held legitimate scientific credentials; Bigelow's ability to attract them suggests his research was taken seriously

Senator Harry Reid's explicit advocacy — Reid secured the AAWSAP appropriation specifically for Bigelow, calling him 'the most knowledgeable person in the country on this subject'; Reid was Senate Majority Leader at the time
60 Minutes interview represents a verifiable on-record primary source — major CBS news program, not a fringe outlet; his statement was made without ambiguity
NIDS produced a peer-reviewed adjacent body of work — Kelleher published in scientific journals on cattle mutilation and anomalous phenomena; not all NIDS research was internal

His institutional network directly enabled the AAWSAP DIRDs — without Bigelow and BAASS, Puthoff and Davis would not have produced the classified DIA documents that are now the most verifiable evidence of exotic physics research in the UAP context
contradicting: Has never provided physical evidence to support his specific ET claims — his conviction is backed by institutional experience and hearsay, not documentable hard evidence he can share
Financial conflict of interest — Bigelow Aerospace is a space technology company; UAP-adjacent credibility may benefit his commercial reputation

Skinwalker Ranch phenomena remain unverified and resisted controlled scientific investigation — many NIDS researchers described frustration at phenomena that appeared to 'respond' to the presence of instruments, making repeatable scientific measurement impossible
AAWSAP scope included some speculative topics (e.g., telepathy, remote viewing, poltergeist phenomena) that go beyond UAP and reduce the credibility of the program as narrowly focused UAP research
Has not testified under oath before Congress on his UAP-specific claims

His connection to the Lazar cluster is indirect and geographical rather than documented — Bigelow was in Las Vegas, Knapp was in Las Vegas; they knew each other and collaborated, but Bigelow was not involved in the Lazar disclosure specifically

associated_people.name: George Knapp
associated_people.relationship: Longtime media partner; NIDS collaborator; co-authored 'Hunt for the Skinwalker' through Kelleher; Knapp was KLAS-TV's primary UAP investigative journalist in Las Vegas - the same media market as Bigelow's operations

associated_people.name: Colm Kelleher
associated_people.role: NIDS Research Director; BAASS Deputy Administrator
associated_people.relationship: NIDS Research Director and BAASS Deputy Administrator; the operational executive who ran both Bigelow's private research organization and the government-contracted AAWSAP program

associated_people.name: Hal Puthoff
associated_people.role: EarthTech International co-founder; NIDS scientific advisor
associated_people.relationship: Scientific advisor to NIDS; primary AAWSAP researcher through EarthTech; their professional relationship spans 25+ years across NIDS, BAASS, and TTSA

associated_people.name: Eric Davis
associated_people.relationship: Visiting research scientist at NIDS; AAWSAP DIRD author through BAASS; Davis's tenure at EarthTech overlapped continuously with Bigelow's research programs

associated_people.name: Harry Reid
associated_people.relationship: U.S. Senate Majority Leader who secured the $22M AAWSAP appropriation for BAASS; Reid called Bigelow the most knowledgeable person in the U.S. on UAP

associated_people.name: Jacques Vallée
associated_people.role: Computer scientist and ufologist; NIDS scientific advisor
associated_people.relationship: NIDS scientific advisor; ufologist and computer scientist who provided theoretical frameworks for the NIDS anomalous phenomena research

disclosures.description: 'Hunt for the Skinwalker' by Colm Kelleher and George Knapp — the first public account of Bigelow's NIDS Skinwalker Ranch investigation. Published commercially and brought Bigelow's research program to mainstream UAP awareness for the first time.

Interview with Lara Logan: explicitly stated 'I'm absolutely convinced' that ETs exist and have visited Earth; described spending 'millions and millions' on research; stated the subject does not make him a curiosity or embarrassment. Aired weeks before the December 2017 NYT AATIP investigation.
BAASS named as the DIA AAWSAP contractor in the NYT AATIP investigation, publicly confirming the government-Bigelow connection for the first time.

Multiple follow-up interviews in which Bigelow elaborated on the NIDS and BAASS research programs, the nature of UAP phenomena, and his views on government involvement. Spoke with Vice, Popular Mechanics, and additional outlets.
BAASS-authored AAWSAP DIRDs partially released via FOIA requests, confirming the scope and nature of government-contracted research conducted under Bigelow's organizations.
